text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, Little Miss Willow sat on a log, sewing a quilt. It was raining gently, so she hummed a soothing tune.

Outside, a squirrel darted from tree to tree collecting acorns. Then it stopped abruptly. Its ears perked up.

"You there!" it squeaked.

Willow looked up, seeing a small owl.

"It sounds like you've gossiped," said the old owl.

She chuckled softly. "Well now, it couldn't possibly interest me. Why don't you tell?"

The squirrel pranced about, shaking leaves.

"Just look," it said. "There are leaves shaped like birds."

Willow pinched her noses. "Oh, they're quite pretty."

"They're owls!" It hooted proudly. "Don't tell me. You think you've got wings?"

Willow laughed. "No chance. I mean, wings would certainly help."

"How about a sharp talon?"

She shook her head. "No."
